DISCOVERY 301, MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES CONTINUED FROM DEC-03-2025 TO DISCOVERY AT JAN-21-2026, 9:00 AM IN DEPT. 301. THE COURT CONCLUDES THAT PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT SATISFIED THEIR OBLIGATION TO MEET AND CONFER IN A GOOD FAITH EFFORT TO RESOLVE THIS DISPUTE. PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER TELEPHONICALLY OR IN PERSON BY DECEMBER 15, 2025. IF ANY ITEMS REMAIN IN DISPUTE, PLAINTIFFS MAY ADVISE THE COURT IN A SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF FILED NO LATER THAN NINE COURT DAYS IN ADVANCE OF THE CONTINUED HEARING DATE. DEFENDANT MAY RESPOND IN A SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF BY NO LATER THAN FIVE COURT DAYS IN ADVANCE OF THE CONTINUED HEARING DATE. THE COURT GIVES NOTICE TO BOTH PARTIES THAT IT MAY IMPOSE SANCTIONS OF UP TO $1000 AT THE CONTINUED HEARING DATE AGAINST ANY PARTY WHOSE DISCOVERY POSITION LACKS SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION OR WHO HAS FAILED TO MEET AND CONFER IN GOOD FAITH. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING) JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/CVA)

calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES
Off Calendar Jan-21-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS KEITH MESSICK, AN INDIVIDUAL, and KI COALE's MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Donald Mahnke, Esq., of Quill & Arrow, LLP, (310) 933-4271, for Plaintiffs.
Deena J. Goodman, Esq., of Squire Patton Boggs, (213) 689-6539, for Defendants.

The subject matter is discussed, specifically regarding the court's orders as discussed at the Dec-03-2025 hearing. At the conclusion of discussion, the subject matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)

MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES
Off Calendar Dec-03-2025 Continued To Jan-21-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as modified to address the discrepancy as to the continued hearing date:

PLAINTIFFS KEITH MESSICK, KI COALE'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES.

The court concludes that Plaintiffs have not satisfied their obligation to meet and confer in a good faith effort to resolve this dispute. The court continues the motion for hearing to January 21, 2026. The court orders the parties to meet and confer telephonically or in person by December 15, 2025. If any items remain in dispute, Plaintiffs may advise the court in a supplemental brief filed no later than nine court days in advance of the continued hearing date. Defendant may respond in a supplemental brief by no later than five court days in advance of the continued hearing date.

The court gives notice to both parties that it may impose sanctions of up to $1000 at the continued hearing date against any party whose discovery position lacks substantial justification or who has failed to meet and confer in good faith.

(see word minutes for complete ruling)

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-29-2025 Set For Trial On Nov-16-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
